jokes.text: Oh. Haven't you heard? The building's going condo.
jokes.setup: Liz thinks Wendy is the cleaning lady
jokes.punchline: Wendy reveals she's actually the real estate agent
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with economic setup-payoff structure

jokes.text: They could've done, 'Geiss screams, son pay!' you know, like, ice cream sundae.
jokes.setup: Serious news story about family lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Liz suggests terrible newspaper pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Liz's awkward attempt at wordplay in serious moment

jokes.text: Holding up one finger to get someone to stop talking... He invented that.
jokes.setup: Jack praising Don Geiss as a great man
jokes.punchline: Claims he invented universal gesture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation perfectly fitting Jack's corporate worship

jokes.text: Geiss also invented the abrupt conversational segue. Talk about your thing now.
jokes.setup: Continuing to praise Don Geiss's inventions
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates the 'invention' while using it
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy gold - Jack demonstrates the segue while claiming Geiss invented it

jokes.text: A walk-in humidor, a lap pool, and a replica of the Irish pub where my grandmother was born.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining what he has instead of a family
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific list ending with bizarre personal detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from luxury to weirdly personal - pure Jack character comedy

jokes.text: Make him an offer he can't refuse. Trademark 1974, Don Geiss.
jokes.setup: Giving Liz real estate advice
jokes.punchline: Attributes Godfather quote to Don Geiss
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the 'Geiss invented everything' running gag with perfect timing

jokes.text: It's like check in at an Italian airport.
jokes.setup: Manhattan real estate has no rules
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific comparison to Italian airports
jokes.explanation: Jack's weirdly specific cultural observations - good character consistency

jokes.text: Were you gonna pee in that jar? I didn't know anyone was here. That's what you use the jars for? You told me that was sun tea. Some of them are sun tea, and some of them were sun tea.
jokes.setup: Liz catches Frank about to pee in a jar
jokes.punchline: Frank's distinction between current and former sun tea
jokes.explanation: Disgusting but brilliantly structured - Frank's logic is perfectly wrong

jokes.text: Some of them are sun tea, and some of them were sun tea.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his jar system
jokes.punchline: Clever past/present tense distinction between tea and urine
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crafted wordplay that's both clever and disgusting. Perfect economy with maximum impact from minimal words. Very quotable.

jokes.text: He gets the jars, and in return he's agreed to let a Yale sweatshirt be our designated fart dampener.
jokes.setup: Explaining the gentleman's agreement about jars
jokes.punchline: Reveals even more disgusting detail about the Yale sweatshirt
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of disgusting writer room behavior with character-specific Yale detail

jokes.text: We'd have a room dog named Meatus.
jokes.setup: What would happen without women in the office
jokes.punchline: Specific gross name for hypothetical office dog
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but fits Lutz's character - anatomical humor is cheap but committed

jokes.text: I will fight my sister tooth and nail to get what I deserve because I, Bertram Geiss, am still daddy's fancy boy.
jokes.setup: Serious legal declaration
jokes.punchline: Undermined by childish 'fancy boy' declaration
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between serious legal language and pathetic self-description

jokes.text: where has this jar been?
jokes.setup: Frank threatening to recycle the pee jars
jokes.punchline: Implies Liz might unknowingly drink from a former urine container
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that extends the jar joke's implications. Dark humor about contamination fits Frank's character.

jokes.text: My name is Teddy Ruxpin. Can you and I be friends?
jokes.setup: Lawyer's rebuttal to serious lawsuit
jokes.punchline: Kathy responds like a children's toy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kathy Geiss moment - childlike response in adult legal context

jokes.text: If he was a monkey, then why was he killed by a monkey?
jokes.setup: Kenneth's denial of evolution
jokes.punchline: Bizarre counter-logic about his grandfather
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's twisted logic is perfectly in character and absurdist

jokes.text: I have these rare kadupul blossoms flown in every morning from Sri Lanka on a private jet. That's the definition of green.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to love the earth
jokes.punchline: Massively wasteful practice described as 'green'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack obliviousness - completely missing the point of environmental concern

jokes.text: Well, they make our shoes and wallets.
jokes.setup: What have children ever done for us?
jokes.punchline: Kenneth matter-of-factly mentions child labor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Kenneth's innocent tone - perfect character contrast

jokes.text: But I'm gonna be grumpy until the end this sentence.
jokes.setup: Kenneth reluctantly agreeing to help
jokes.punchline: Self-aware statement about his own emotional timeline
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about Kenneth's limited emotional range

jokes.text: I have a view because that dentist blew up his townhouse.
jokes.setup: Brian showing off his apartment's view
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of violent explosion as real estate benefit
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with casual delivery of shocking information

jokes.text: I can't shut the flue, so the apartment smells like Burger King all day and Cinnabon all night.
jokes.setup: Apartment has annoying fireplace problem
jokes.punchline: Specific smell schedule from nearby chain restaurants
jokes.explanation: Specific and relatable urban living complaint with good detail

jokes.text: and their imaginary husband, astronaut Mike Dexter.
jokes.setup: Complaining about yuppies with imaginary families
jokes.punchline: Specific ridiculous name for imaginary husband
jokes.explanation: Absurd but memorable name that will become important later

jokes.text: based on your shoes and teeth, i'm guessing you can't either
jokes.setup: Brian suggesting they team up financially
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Liz's economic status via appearance
jokes.explanation: Mean but accurate dig at Liz's perpetual financial struggles

jokes.text: Like check in at an Italian sex party.
jokes.setup: Jack repeating his 'no rules' comparison
jokes.punchline: Escalates from airport to sex party
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalation but feels slightly forced

jokes.text: Oh, you ancient bitch!
jokes.setup: Mrs. Gerstein says her heart is getting stronger
jokes.punchline: Jack's frustrated outburst at her survival
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack reaction - inappropriately honest and darkly funny

jokes.text: I know it's my turn to do the dishes, but I'm in character, and if you make me do the dishes I will kill myself!
jokes.setup: Jenna demonstrating drama queen behavior
jokes.punchline: Absurd threat over minor household chore
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna escalation from mundane to melodramatic

jokes.text: Our topical cold open is about Omarosa borrowing Bjork's swan dress.
jokes.setup: Liz assuring Jack about their comedy content
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical pop culture mashup
jokes.explanation: Absurd but feels like a random reference rather than earned comedy

jokes.text: It's 'take your black kid to work day.'
jokes.setup: Tracy Jr.'s presence at the office
jokes.punchline: Tracy's casual renaming of the diversity initiative
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy directness about race. The casual delivery and renaming fits his character's perspective.

jokes.text: Oh, no, Vanessa into a concert! Oh, no, Rudy and I are making a sandwich for 25 minutes!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining Cosby Show expectations
jokes.punchline: Mocks the mundane conflicts of the show
jokes.explanation: Good parody of sitcom formula with Tracy's characteristic exaggeration

jokes.text: Bitches can be tricky.
jokes.setup: Tracy Jr. giving relationship advice to Jack
jokes.punchline: Child using adult language inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Shock humor from inappropriate child language - functional but cheap

jokes.text: Damn it! I ain't live like this! I'm getting a vasectomy too!
jokes.setup: Tracy frustrated by having his son around
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to temporary inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Tracy's characteristic overreaction but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: this one's for my erotic massager, and this one's for something personal.
jokes.setup: Jenna listing what her chargers are for
jokes.punchline: Implies the erotic massager isn't the most personal item
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with perfect Jenna oversharing

jokes.text: I should warn you that general anesthesia can cause powerful hallucinations. So I highly recommend it.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving medical warning
jokes.punchline: Recommends dangerous option for fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman moment - medical incompetence disguised as helpfulness

jokes.text: because I'll have a big breakfast waiting for you.
jokes.setup: Warning not to eat before surgery
jokes.punchline: Offers breakfast immediately after telling them not to eat
jokes.explanation: Good Dr. Spaceman incompetence but less sharp than previous joke

jokes.text: I will put on a wedding dress and jump in front of a subway!
jokes.setup: Liz doing drama queen act
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific threat with wedding dress detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but Liz doing drama feels less natural than Jenna

jokes.text: Astronaut Mike Dexter.
jokes.setup: Gay Brian asking about boyfriend's name
jokes.punchline: Liz uses the same ridiculous name Brian mentioned earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Liz wasn't really listening earlier

jokes.text: Drama is like gay man Gatorade. It replenishes their electrolytes.
jokes.setup: Explaining why drama wouldn't drive Brian out
jokes.punchline: Specific sports drink comparison for gay men and drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity in the comparison - very quotable Jenna wisdom

jokes.text: I cannot tell an amazing strip club story.
jokes.setup: Tracy's ongoing frustration with having his son around
jokes.punchline: Strip club story becomes the running thread
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag that will pay off later

jokes.text: are they just gonna put that stupid green peacock in the corner of the screen?
jokes.setup: Liz asking about NBC's green week efforts
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about NBC's actual green week branding
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on NBC's actual promotional practices

jokes.text: You lied to me, Bill Cosby! You lied to me!
jokes.setup: Tracy going under anesthesia
jokes.punchline: His anger at Bill Cosby comes out during surgery
jokes.explanation: Great callback with emotional escalation under anesthesia

jokes.text: That's why my life is not like the Cosby Show. I only have boys, and boys are disgusting!
jokes.setup: Tracy's hallucination with Cosby family
jokes.punchline: Realizes the real difference between his life and the show
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation in the hallucination sequence

jokes.text: Don't snip my vas deferens!
jokes.setup: Tracy panicking during surgery
jokes.punchline: Scientifically accurate panic about the procedure
jokes.explanation: Medical accuracy makes it funnier than generic panic

jokes.text: Even if he's gay!
jokes.setup: Fake boyfriend warning about other men
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges Brian's sexuality while still being jealous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but feels like setup for the real punchline

jokes.text: Like Warren Moon must've felt back in 1995!
jokes.setup: Expressing anger and jealousy
jokes.punchline: Obscure football reference that nobody gets
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific sports reference that Tracy would know

jokes.text: A gay hipster cop. You're an interesting guy, Brian.
jokes.setup: Police show up during the fake fight
jokes.punchline: Liz's oddly casual observation about Brian's social circles
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to absurd situation

jokes.text: That junk is priceless.
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of Don Geiss with his kids
jokes.punchline: Child innocently comments on adult anatomy
jokes.explanation: Double entendre from child but feels somewhat cheap

jokes.text: 'T' is for terrific, 'R' is for rad, 'A' is for awesome, 'C'... 'cause he's my dad.
jokes.setup: Tracy Jr. reading his acrostic poem
jokes.punchline: Breaks the pattern with simple sentiment instead of adjective
jokes.explanation: Sweet and funny - breaks expected pattern in touching way

jokes.text: Why doesn't that say 'hero'? That feels like a real missed opportunity.
jokes.setup: Kenneth quoting NBC's green week slogan
jokes.punchline: Liz points out obvious rhyme they missed
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on corporate messaging failure

jokes.text: What's a trash barrel but a big salad bowl filled with trash?
jokes.setup: Defending eating from trash
jokes.punchline: Reframes disgusting behavior as logical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frank logic - disgusting but undeniably reasonable

jokes.text: You look like a turtle who lost its shell.
jokes.setup: Kenneth criticizing Frank
jokes.punchline: Frank's oddly specific insult about Kenneth's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Frank insult - specific and visual

jokes.text: Why did I sell my Delorean to Mr. T?
jokes.setup: Tracy wanting to go back in time
jokes.punchline: Reveals he owned and sold Back to the Future car to A-Team star
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - casually mentions impossible celebrity transaction

jokes.text: I hope he makes mean across helmet so I don't get hurt playing across!
jokes.setup: Tracy still groggy from anesthesia
jokes.punchline: Confuses acrostic with lacrosse in drug-addled state
jokes.explanation: Good callback with drug-induced confusion

jokes.text: You used it to kick a gay cop out of his apartment.
jokes.setup: Frank defending his environmental efforts
jokes.punchline: Casually points out Liz's morally worse action
jokes.explanation: Good moral comparison that makes Frank look better

jokes.text: Especially if they've had their boobies sneezed on by a tiger.
jokes.setup: Tracy refusing to tell his strip club story
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals bizarre detail anyway
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to running gag - absurd detail while claiming restraint

jokes.text: If you want to go quickly, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
jokes.setup: Al Gore giving environmental wisdom
jokes.punchline: Admits he made up the African proverb
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about fake wisdom attribution

jokes.text: A whale is in trouble. I have to go.
jokes.setup: Al Gore giving environmental advice
jokes.punchline: Suddenly leaves for vague environmental emergency
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist exit line fitting Al Gore's environmental crusader image

jokes.text: Smell my flowers, Kenneth.
jokes.setup: Jack showing off his environmental efforts
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately intimate command to Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but fitting end to Jack's environmental phoniness
